title: Settings page lacks tab/section navigation for discoverability
body:
ISSUE: SettingsPage.tsx (line 6-22) renders all settings sections in a single vertical scrollable column with no hierarchical navigation (API Keys, Provider Preferences, Appearance). Users must scroll through a flat document to find specific settings categories, creating poor discoverability and information architecture throughout the preferences interface.

CURRENT STATE: Linear layout with no tab bar, sidebar, collapsible sections, or breadcrumbs. All three major sections are always visible in the DOM, making it difficult to focus on one task at a time.

REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add a left-side nav panel or top tab bar listing major setting groups (API Keys, Providers, Appearance, Advanced, Data, Accessibility)
- Each group should be independently scrollable with active state indicator and badge counts (e.g., "2 keys configured")
- Mobile: collapse to dropdown menu or stacked button tabs at top
- Keyboard navigation: arrow keys navigate between tabs, Ctrl+Tab cycles forward, Shift+Ctrl+Tab backward, focus trap within section content
- Save active tab to localStorage for session persistence across app restarts

IMPACT: Users with many settings (e.g., 5+ API keys across multiple providers, multiple custom model configurations per provider) struggle to locate what they need. Settings feel like a flat document rather than a professional preferences panel. New users don't know what settings exist beyond the fold. This reduces engagement with advanced features and increases support burden significantly.

FILES: src/renderer/src/modules/settings/SettingsPage.tsx:6-22, src/renderer/src/modules/settings/ApiKeyManager.tsx, src/renderer/src/modules/settings/ProviderPreferences.tsx, src/renderer/src/modules/settings/ThemeToggle.tsx

title: API key validation lacks provider-specific format feedback
body:
ISSUE: ApiKeyManager.tsx (line 61-78) accepts any non-empty string as an API key and only provides minimal validation feedback. However, Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, xAI, and Perplexity all have distinct key format requirements, character lengths, and validation constraints that should be enforced client-side before submission.

CURRENT STATE: KeyRow component shows a text input field. QuickSetup.tsx (line 61-65) checks only basic prefix matching (e.g., 'sk-ant-' for Anthropic, 'sk-' for OpenAI) without comprehensive format validation of the full key string.

MISSING VALIDATION:
- Real-time client-side validation on paste: check key length (Anthropic ~48 chars, OpenAI ~48+ alphanumeric), character sets, provider-specific patterns and encoding
- Format guidance in placeholder or persistent helper text (e.g., "Anthropic keys: start with sk-ant-, 48+ characters")
- Error message distinguishing "wrong format detected" vs "key rejected by provider API" after test
- Visual indicator (yellow warning icon) for keys that match format but haven't been tested yet
- Show estimated key type before user submits ("Looks like an Anthropic key")

IMPACT: Users paste malformed keys (e.g., including extra whitespace, env var format like $SK_ANT=..., or truncated copies), then encounter cryptic "test failed" errors without understanding root cause. This increases support burden and frustration significantly. Users disable auto-save and blame the app rather than their input. Compliance-critical when users manage multiple enterprise keys.

FILES: src/renderer/src/modules/settings/ApiKeyManager.tsx:61-78, src/renderer/src/shell/QuickSetup.tsx:61-65

title: Model selection UX missing deprecation and context window info
body:
ISSUE: ProviderPreferences.tsx (line 52-77) allows users to manually enter arbitrary model identifiers via a plain text input field with zero validation, deprecation warnings, or context window information. Users cannot easily discover current, supported models or understand their capabilities and costs.

CURRENT STATE: Text input with a default model name (e.g., 'claude-sonnet-4-6'). Users can type any string; the app only fails at runtime when the provider API rejects the unknown model. No helper text explains which models are current, recommended, expensive, or context limits.

REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION:
- Replace text input with curated dropdown list of CURRENT, actively-supported models per provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, xAI, Perplexity)
- Maintain a model catalog with metadata: context_window, cost_per_mtok, release_date, deprecation_status
- Mark deprecated models with red warning badge: "claude-3.5-opus deprecated — switch to claude-3.6-sonnet (200k tokens, $3/mtok)"
- Show context window in parentheses for each option: "claude-3.6-sonnet (200k tokens)", "gpt-4o (128k tokens)"
- Disable model selection inputs until a test-passed key exists for that provider
- Add info icon with tooltip: "Model must be supported by your API key tier and rate limits"
- Store model metadata in constants.ts and update monthly

IMPACT: Users unknowingly select deprecated, old, or extremely expensive models. Vague placeholder text ("gpt-4o") doesn't clarify if it's a recommendation or the current maximum available model. Errors only surface at runtime ("unknown model") after the user has already activated the setting and run a query. Power users cannot compare models side-by-side. This impacts cost control and feature access significantly.

FILES: src/renderer/src/modules/settings/ProviderPreferences.tsx:52-77, src/renderer/src/lib/constants.ts:28-34

title: Theme toggle lacks system preference follow and custom accent
body:
ISSUE: ThemeToggle.tsx (line 6-39) implements a binary dark/light toggle button with no option to follow OS theme preferences or customize the brand accent color. uiStore.ts (line 26, 32) stores only 'dark' | 'light' with no auto or accent color support.

CURRENT STATE: Users manually toggle between exactly two hard-coded themes. The accent color (#cc785c warm orange in light mode, #da7756 in dark mode) is baked into globals.css with no UI to change it. When user has OS set to dark mode and app set to light, they must manually switch every session.

REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION:
- Replace toggle button with dropdown: Auto (follow OS) | Light | Dark
- When Auto is selected, listen to prefers-color-scheme media query and window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').addListener()
- Auto-detect system.nativeTheme.shouldUseDarkColors (Electron) on startup and toggle appropriately
- Add custom accent color picker section: hue slider (0-360) or hex input field to customize the warm palette
- Store custom accent hex as CSS variable in prefs.ts alongside theme: { theme: 'auto'|'dark'|'light', accentColor: '#cc785c' }
- Apply custom accent as --color-accent CSS variable on DOM load from saved preference
- Show current accent color preview swatch next to picker, update in real-time as user adjusts
- Provide preset accent options (warm, cool, emerald, rose) for quick selection

IMPACT: Power users want coherent system theme support but can't configure it, leading to accessibility and usability friction. Brand consistency demands custom accent for white-label deployments but there's no UI for it. Every user is locked into one orange palette. This prevents theme personalization and reduces app fidelity for enterprise customers.

FILES: src/renderer/src/modules/settings/ThemeToggle.tsx:6-39, src/renderer/src/stores/uiStore.ts:17-47, src/renderer/src/styles/globals.css:1-45, src/main/services/prefs.ts:1-56

title: Layout reset UI unclear on scope (per-module vs global)
body:
ISSUE: useLayout.ts (line 70) provides a reset() function that resets dashboard layout to initial state, but there's no UI control in SettingsPage or within module dashboards to let users trigger or understand reset scope (per-module vs application-wide).

CURRENT STATE: Layout save/reset logic exists only in code. SettingsPage renders zero layout management controls. Users accidentally drag widgets into bad positions and have no self-service recovery path beyond restarting the app or manually undoing drags. Users don't understand whether layouts are module-isolated (Finance layout independent from Health) or global.

REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add Advanced or Layouts section in Settings with a list of saved layouts per module (Finance, Health, Sports, PDF, Assistant)
- For each saved layout: display name, creation date, and "Reset to default" button with confirmation dialog
- Confirmation text: "Clear all widget positions and sizes for Finance module — this cannot be undone"
- Add optional "Global reset" checkbox: "Reset all modules to defaults at once" to batch-reset all dashboard layouts in one action
- Show layout save timestamp: "Default layout (created Oct 2025)" alongside any custom named layouts
- Hide draft/unsaved layouts in the list (only show layouts with is_default=1 in the database)
- Add info icon with explanation: "Each module's dashboard layout is saved separately and restored on app restart"

IMPACT: Users cannot recover from accidentally disrupting their layouts without full app reset or manual work. Uncertainty about scope reduces confidence in making dashboard changes. Layout management is hidden, making it seem like a bug rather than a feature. Enterprise users managing multiple modules across teams cannot reset layouts selectively.

FILES: src/renderer/src/hooks/useLayout.ts:24-72, src/renderer/src/modules/settings/SettingsPage.tsx:6-22

title: Missing import/export settings bundle for device migration
body:
ISSUE: No mechanism exists to export user preferences (API keys, provider choices, theme, layout) to a portable file for backup or transfer to a new machine, forcing users to manually reconfigure everything on device migration.

CURRENT STATE: prefs.ts stores preferences as JSON locally using createJsonStore(). Keys are stored in OS keychain (macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager). There's no export/import UI, no data portability feature, and no way to backup settings before OS reinstall.

REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add Settings > Data section with two buttons: "Export Settings" and "Import Settings"
- Export creates daja-settings-[ISO-timestamp].json containing:
  - aiByModule, modelByProvider, theme (from prefs.ts)
  - All saved layout configs per module (from database: layouts table)
  - Metadata: app_version, export_date, module_list
  - **EXCLUDE API keys** from export — warn user: "API keys are NOT included in export for security. Store them separately or re-paste after import"
- Import dialog workflow:
  - File picker for daja-settings-*.json files
  - Show preview: "This will import: 5 layouts, 3 providers, dark theme"
  - Confirmation modal: "This will overwrite your current preferences — continue?"
  - Progress bar during import (if large layout configs)
  - Success toast: "5 settings imported successfully"
  - Error toast if file is corrupted: "Invalid settings file"

IMPACT: Users cannot backup their complex, custom layouts and preferences before OS reinstall, machine failure, or migration to new computer. No cross-machine preference sync (e.g., work/home desktop). Users spend hours reconfiguring dashboards after setup. Enterprise users cannot standardize settings across teams. Backup/restore is a basic feature expectation in modern applications.

FILES: src/main/services/prefs.ts:1-56, src/renderer/src/modules/settings/SettingsPage.tsx:6-22, src/renderer/src/hooks/usePrefs.ts:1-40

title: Unsaved changes not indicated when editing settings
body:
ISSUE: ApiKeyManager, ProviderPreferences, and ThemeToggle save changes immediately (onBlur, onClick) but provide no visual indication of pending saves, loading state, or confirmation that changes were successfully persisted or failed.

CURRENT STATE: ProviderPreferences.tsx (line 67-69) saves model changes on blur silently with zero feedback. ApiKeyManager.tsx (line 77) saves API keys via mutation but doesn't show any loading spinner or success/error state in the UI. User cannot tell if an edit was saved, is still saving, or was rejected by the backend.

REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add a floating "Unsaved changes" banner at top of SettingsPage when any field is edited but pending save
- For each form control (input, select, color picker):
  - Show inline spinner icon while save is in-flight (next to the field or as background tint)
  - Show green checkmark briefly (1.5–2 seconds) when saved successfully, then fade
  - Show red error icon if save fails; keep banner visible and red until user retries
  - Display error message in banner: "Failed to save model — API error. Retry?"
- Warn on navigation: "You have unsaved settings — leave anyway?" if Unsaved banner is present
- Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl+S to save all pending fields immediately
- Toast notification on batch save: "Settings saved successfully"

IMPACT: Users don't know if a model change was saved or is still pending. They close the app thinking their custom layout is persisted, only to find defaults restored on next launch. Silent failures breed distrust. Users make the same edits repeatedly thinking the app didn't register them. This increases frustration, reduces confidence in settings, and creates false bug reports.

FILES: src/renderer/src/modules/settings/ApiKeyManager.tsx:75-78, src/renderer/src/modules/settings/ProviderPreferences.tsx:67-69, src/renderer/src/modules/settings/SettingsPage.tsx:6-22, src/renderer/src/hooks/usePrefs.ts:12-39

title: QuickSetup lacks progress, skip option, and validation messaging
body:
ISSUE: QuickSetup.tsx (line 37-226) is a good first-run experience but is missing progressive onboarding UX patterns: visible progress indicator, explicit skip button, and detailed validation error messages.

CURRENT STATE: Single-screen form with no progress bar or step indicator. Error messages are terse (line 58: "Paste an API key", line 62: "Key doesn't look like...", line 80: "Failed to save key"). The "skip for now" affordance (line 217-222) is a gray box of small text, not a prominent button, so users miss it.

REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add 3-step progress bar at top: "Step 1: Choose Provider | Step 2: Paste Key | Step 3: Verify (optional)" with visual progress (1/3, 2/3, 3/3)
- Improve validation error messages with context:
  - Prefix mismatch: "This looks like an OpenAI key (starts with 'sk-') but you selected Anthropic. Paste an Anthropic key (starts with 'sk-ant-') or change provider?"
  - Format validation: "Anthropic keys should be 48+ alphanumeric characters. Yours is 25 chars — check you didn't paste a partial key."
  - On test failure: "Key format is valid but was rejected by Anthropic API. Check it's not expired, revoked, or rate-limited. Log in to console.anthropic.com to verify."
- Add explicit "Skip & use app anyway" button (not text in a box) with modal confirmation: "You can add an API key anytime in Settings. Continue without key?"
- After successful save, show "Ready to use!" confirmation screen with 2-second countdown before auto-redirect to Launchpad
- On return to QuickSetup (e.g., via Settings > Manage Keys > Start Setup), detect existing keys and show: "You already have 2 keys configured. Add another, reconfigure, or go back?"

IMPACT: New users don't understand validation errors and may quit mid-setup thinking the app is broken. The skip option is easy to miss, causing frustration. Users feel lost without progress indication. Repeat visits to QuickSetup aren't handled gracefully, creating confusion about whether they're re-running setup or starting fresh.

FILES: src/renderer/src/shell/QuickSetup.tsx:37-226, src/renderer/src/shell/QuickSetup.tsx:54-84

title: Privacy/telemetry controls and data retention options missing
body:
ISSUE: No Settings section exists for privacy preferences: crash reporting opt-out, telemetry toggle, analytics control, or data retention configuration. Users have no visibility or control over what data leaves their machine.

CURRENT STATE: SettingsPage and prefs.ts don't mention or store privacy settings. No opt-out UI for analytics, crash reports, or debug logging. Users cannot set data retention policies or view what's being collected.

REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add Privacy & Data section in Settings with these toggles and dropdowns:
  - "Send crash reports to Anthropic" (default: on) — explains they help fix bugs
  - "Send feature usage analytics" (default: on) — explains they're anonymous
  - "Delete debug logs after X days" (dropdown: never | 7 | 30 | 90 days, default: 30)
  - "Delete cached API responses after X days" (dropdown: never | 7 | 30 | 90 days, default: never)
  - "Delete unused API keys from keychain history" toggle (default: off)
- Add explanation text: "Crash reports help us fix bugs and never include your data, models, or API keys. Analytics are anonymous and never personally identifiable."
- Add "View privacy policy" link pointing to legal documentation
- Add "Reset all app data" button with strong warning modal: "This will permanently delete all layouts, preferences, API keys, cached responses, and debug logs. This cannot be undone. Proceed?"
- Store privacy choices in prefs.ts and respect user selections at runtime
- Implement automatic data cleanup jobs based on retention settings

IMPACT: Users have zero control over what's sent out of their machine, creating trust deficit and compliance risk. Users cannot verify data isn't leaking. Enterprise customers cannot meet data governance requirements. GDPR/privacy regulation compliance gap. Users distrust the app when they can't see privacy controls.

FILES: src/renderer/src/modules/settings/SettingsPage.tsx:6-22, src/main/services/prefs.ts:1-56, src/renderer/src/hooks/usePrefs.ts:1-40

title: Accessibility settings (font size, motion, contrast) not implemented
body:
ISSUE: No accessibility settings exist for font sizing, respecting prefers-reduced-motion media query, or high contrast mode. uiStore.ts and SettingsPage don't include accessibility controls. Hard-coded Tailwind classes prevent dynamic scaling.

CURRENT STATE: Font sizes are hard-coded throughout as Tailwind classes (text-xs, text-sm, text-base, etc.). No reduced motion media query respect — animations play regardless of user OS settings. No high-contrast variant of the theme. Users with low vision or vestibular disorders cannot use the app effectively.

REQUIRED IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add Accessibility section in Settings with controls:
  - "Font size" slider: 80% to 150% with labeled steps (Small | Normal | Large | Extra Large)
  - "Respect system motion preferences" toggle — when on, checks prefers-reduced-motion and disables animations
  - "High contrast mode" toggle — increases all color contrast ratios to WCAG AAA (7:1), increases border widths, removes shadows
- Store { fontScale: 100, reduceMotion: false, highContrast: false } in prefs.ts
- Update uiStore.ts to include fontScale, reduceMotion, highContrast state
- Apply --font-scale CSS variable: calc(1rem * var(--font-scale) / 100) to body in globals.css
- Apply transition-duration: 0s when --reduce-motion is true
- Apply high-contrast class to html which overrides color variables with higher-contrast alternatives
- Show "Test" button next to each setting to live-preview changes in real-time
- Persist choices across sessions

IMPACT: Users with vision or vestibular disabilities cannot use the app. WCAG 2.1 AA/AAA compliance gap excludes users with low vision, color blindness, and motion sensitivity. Users with vestibular issues experience nausea from animations. App is inaccessible to ~15% of population with disabilities. Legal/compliance risk under WCAG and ADA.

FILES: src/renderer/src/stores/uiStore.ts:1-47, src/renderer/src/modules/settings/SettingsPage.tsx:6-22, src/renderer/src/styles/globals.css:1-150
